name: Dex Identity Provider API
what_it_does: Open-source OpenID Connect (OIDC) identity provider and OAuth2 server — acts as a federated identity bridge that connects to upstream identity providers (LDAP, SAML, GitHub, Google, etc.) and issues standardized OIDC tokens to downstream applications.
use_cases: Federating multiple identity sources (LDAP, SAML, GitHub) behind a single OIDC endpoint for Kubernetes clusters
Adding OIDC to legacy applications that only support LDAP or SAML via protocol translation
Self-hosted identity provider for air-gapped environments needing OAuth2/OIDC without SaaS dependency
Kubernetes RBAC authentication via OIDC tokens issued by Dex from upstream AD/LDAP
Connecting internal tools (Grafana, ArgoCD, Vault) to corporate identity via OIDC federation
not_for: End-user-facing authentication product (Dex is infrastructure — use Keycloak or Auth0 for user-facing auth)
Teams without Kubernetes or infrastructure expertise (complex to operate)
Consumer apps needing user registration, password reset, and profile management
best_when: An infrastructure team needs to federate corporate identity (LDAP/SAML) to OIDC-speaking applications and Kubernetes clusters in a self-hosted, cloud-native environment.
avoid_when: You need user-facing login pages, password management, or a managed auth service — Dex is a protocol bridge, not a full auth platform.

auth.notes: gRPC API for admin operations (create/manage clients, connectors). OIDC standard flows for token issuance. TLS client certificates for gRPC admin API. OAuth2 with PKCE for user auth flows. Highly configurable — auth model depends on deployment.

pricing.paid_tiers: No paid tier; Red Hat/CNCF community project
pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 open source. CNCF sandbox project. Originally by CoreOS (acquired by Red Hat). Widely used in Kubernetes ecosystem. No managed hosting — fully self-hosted only. Strong Kubernetes community adoption.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — self-hosted; depends on upstream IdP

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: gRPC API for admin operations (client management, connector config). Standard OIDC discovery endpoint. REST API limited to OIDC standard endpoints. gRPC protobuf definitions available. Primarily configured via YAML — API is supplementary to static config.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: OIDC token requests create new tokens. Client creation idempotent by client ID. Connector creation idempotent. Password grants idempotent for same credentials.
known_agent_gotchas: Primarily configured via YAML config file — gRPC API is supplementary, not primary interface
gRPC admin API requires TLS client certificate — complex setup vs REST API tools
Upstream IdP latency affects all token issuance — Dex adds a hop to every auth flow
Storage backend (etcd, PostgreSQL, memory) must be chosen at deploy time — affects HA setup

security_score_components.security_notes: Apache 2.0 open source — fully auditable Go codebase. OIDC/OAuth2 standards compliance. mTLS for admin API. Self-hosted gives full data control. No formal certifications. CNCF sandbox — community security reviews. Widely used in production Kubernetes deployments.
